subject: Mozi Minute: Lifestyle Risk vs Business Risk
body:
Words I like: Poor people are impressed by stuff. Rich people are impressed by results. Don't outspend. Outwork.

Your cash has to feed something. It either feeds a growing liability - your lifestyle - or a growing asset - your business.

Mozi Minute: Lifestyle Risk vs Business Risk

You cannot take maximum risk in both your lifestyle and your business.

Most entrepreneurs don't understand this.

They make their first $20k/month and immediately upgrade to a luxury apartment, new car, and expensive dinners.

Then they wonder why their business stops growing.

Here's the truth: You want to take as little lifestyle risk as possible so you can take as much business risk as possible.

Your personal spending caps your business growth.

Real Examples:

Elon Musk got his PayPal payday and slept on his friends' couches while starting SpaceX and Tesla.

I was splitting a room with another guy in a house with 5 roommates, sleeping on a mattress on the floor, while taking home $20k/month.

Sam Walton drove a pickup truck and lived in a modest house even after becoming a billionaire.

The Trade:

Every dollar you spend on lifestyle is a dollar you can't invest in:
- Better talent
- More marketing
- New products
- Expanding capacity
- Building systems

Bottom Line:
Keep your lifestyle lean. Maximize business risk.

Talk soon,
- Alex
